label: Create Task
description:
Create a new task in an Asana project (POST /tasks). Use when the user wants to add a new task, to-do, or work item to an Asana project.

Example response:
```json
{"gid": "12345", "name": "Design review", "resource_type": "task", "completed": false, "due_on": "2024-03-01", "assignee": {"gid": "67890", "name": "Alice"}}
```

paramFields.name: projectGid
paramFields.label: Project GID
paramFields.description: The GID of the project to create the task in. Use List Projects to find project GIDs.

paramFields.name: name
paramFields.label: Task Name
paramFields.description: Name of the task to create.

paramFields.name: notes
paramFields.label: Notes
paramFields.description: Plain-text notes / description for the task.

paramFields.name: dueOn
paramFields.label: Due Date
paramFields.description: Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

paramFields.name: assignee
paramFields.label: Assignee
paramFields.description: Assignee specified as a user GID or email address.

label: Get Task
description:
Get the full details of a single Asana task (GET /tasks/{task_gid}). Use when the user wants to view all information about a specific task including notes, subtasks, and custom fields.

Example response:
```json
{"gid": "12345", "name": "Design review", "notes": "Review the mockups", "completed": false, "due_on": "2024-03-01", "assignee": {"name": "Alice"}}
```

paramFields.name: taskGid
paramFields.label: Task GID
paramFields.description: The GID of the task to retrieve.

label: List Projects
description:
List projects in an Asana workspace (GET /projects?workspace={gid}). Use when the user needs to find project GIDs before listing or creating tasks.

Example response:
```json
[{"gid": "12345", "name": "Q1 Launch", "resource_type": "project"}]
```

paramFields.name: workspaceGid
paramFields.label: Workspace GID
paramFields.description: The GID of the workspace to list projects from. Use List Workspaces to find workspace GIDs.

paramFields.name: limit
paramFields.label: Limit
paramFields.description: Maximum number of projects to return (1–100).

label: List Tasks
description:
List tasks in an Asana project (GET /tasks?project={gid}). Use when the user wants to see all tasks, check completion status, or review upcoming work in a project.

Example response:
```json
[{"gid": "12345", "name": "Design review", "completed": false, "due_on": "2024-03-01", "assignee": {"name": "Alice"}}]
```

paramFields.name: projectGid
paramFields.label: Project GID
paramFields.description: The GID of the project to list tasks from. Use List Projects to find project GIDs.

paramFields.name: limit
paramFields.label: Limit
paramFields.description: Maximum number of tasks to return (1–100).

label: List Workspaces
description:
List all workspaces accessible by the authenticated Asana user (GET /workspaces). Use when the user needs to discover workspace GIDs before listing projects or tasks.

Example response:
```json
[{"gid": "12345", "name": "My Company", "resource_type": "workspace"}]
```

label: Update Task

Update an existing Asana task (PUT /tasks/{task_gid}). Use when the user wants to modify a task's name, notes, due date, completion status, or assignee.

```json
{"gid": "12345", "name": "Updated task", "completed": true, "due_on": "2024-03-01", "assignee": {"name": "Alice"}}
```

label: Get Query Results
description:
Get results for a CloudWatch Logs Insights query (GetQueryResults). Use after cloudwatch.start_query to poll for log query results. Call with the `queryId` returned from start_query. Check `status` — possible values: Running, Scheduled, Complete, Failed, Cancelled, Timeout, Unknown.

Example response:
```json
{"status": "Complete", "results": [{"@timestamp": "2025-04-07 08:00:00", "@message": "INFO: request processed"}], "resultCount": 1, "statistics": {"recordsMatched": 150, "recordsScanned": 5000}}
```

paramFields.name: region
paramFields.label: AWS Region
paramFields.description: AWS region where the query was started

paramFields.name: queryId
paramFields.label: Query ID
paramFields.description: The queryId returned from cloudwatch.start_query.

label: Start Log Insights Query
description:
Start a CloudWatch Logs Insights query (StartQuery). Use when the user wants to search or analyze logs across one or more log groups. Call with comma-separated `logGroupNames`, a Logs Insights `queryString`, and a `startTime` (ISO 8601 or Unix epoch seconds); `endTime` defaults to now. Returns a queryId — poll with cloudwatch.get_query_results until status is "Complete".

Example response:
```json
{"queryId": "12ab3456-12ab-123a-789e-1234567890ab"}
```

paramFields.name: region
paramFields.label: AWS Region
paramFields.description: AWS region where the log groups reside (e.g. us-east-1, eu-west-1)

paramFields.name: logGroupNames
paramFields.label: Log Group Names
paramFields.description: Comma-separated list of CloudWatch log group names to query.

paramFields.name: queryString
paramFields.label: Query
paramFields.description: CloudWatch Logs Insights query string. Uses the Logs Insights query syntax.

paramFields.name: startTime
paramFields.label: Start Time
paramFields.description: Query start time as ISO 8601 string or Unix epoch seconds. Required.

paramFields.name: endTime
paramFields.label: End Time
paramFields.description: Query end time as ISO 8601 string or Unix epoch seconds. Defaults to now.

paramFields.name: limit
paramFields.label: Limit
paramFields.description: Maximum number of log events to return (1–10000).

label: AI Agent
description: LLM agent that can iteratively call other actions as tools to accomplish a task. Supports parallel tool execution, conversation truncation, and native batch processing via OpenAI/Anthropic.

paramFields.name: model
paramFields.label: Model
paramFields.description: LLM model to use for the agent

paramFields.name: taskPrompt
paramFields.label: Task / Goal
paramFields.description: The main task or goal for the agent. Supports {{ expression }} templating.
paramFields.placeholder: Describe what the agent should accomplish...

paramFields.name: systemPrompt
paramFields.label: System Prompt
paramFields.description: System-level instructions for the agent's behavior.
paramFields.placeholder: Optional: Additional context or behavior instructions...

paramFields.name: maxIterations
paramFields.label: Max Iterations
paramFields.description: Maximum number of agent iterations (1-200)

paramFields.name: stopCondition
paramFields.label: Stop Condition
paramFields.description: When the agent should stop executing

paramFields.name: temperature
paramFields.label: Temperature
paramFields.description: Controls randomness (0.0 to 2.0)

paramFields.name: maxTokens
paramFields.label: Max Tokens
paramFields.description: Maximum tokens per LLM call

paramFields.name: toolTimeoutMs
paramFields.label: Tool Timeout (ms)
paramFields.description: Maximum time in milliseconds for each tool execution

paramFields.name: maxConversationTokens
paramFields.label: Max Conversation Tokens
paramFields.description: Token budget for conversation history before truncation

paramFields.name: enableParallelTools
paramFields.label: Enable Parallel Tools
paramFields.description: Execute multiple tool calls in parallel when the LLM requests them

paramFields.name: useBatchProcessing
paramFields.label: Batch Processing
paramFields.description: When enabled, the initial prompt is submitted via the provider's batch API for asynchronous processing. The flow will pause until the batch completes. Only the first iteration uses batch; subsequent iterations run synchronously so the tool loop can continue.

label: If / Else
description: Conditional branching — evaluates a JavaScript expression against incoming upstream data and routes execution to either the True or False output branch. Upstream node outputs are available as local variables by their reference ID (e.g. `user_data.age >= 18`). The input data is passed through unchanged to the active branch.

paramFields.name: expression
paramFields.label: Condition
paramFields.description: JavaScript expression evaluated against incoming data. Upstream node outputs are available as variables by their reference ID.

label: Input
description: Define a flow input variable with an optional default value. Use as the entry point of a flow to accept runtime data. If the caller supplies the named variable, that value is used; otherwise the configured default is emitted.

paramFields.name: variableName
paramFields.label: Variable Name
paramFields.description: Flow input key to read from runtime inputs

paramFields.name: defaultValue
paramFields.label: Default Value
paramFields.description: Fallback value when runtime input is missing (JSON allowed)

label: JavaScript
description: Transform and process data using JavaScript in a sandboxed QuickJS runtime. Use when you need to filter, map, reshape, or compute values from upstream node outputs. Upstream variables are available by their reference ID; `$input` holds the full context object. Single expressions auto-return; use explicit `return` for multi-line code.

paramFields.name: code
paramFields.label: JavaScript Code
paramFields.description: JavaScript to execute. Upstream data is available as local variables by reference ID. Use `return` for multi-line; single expressions auto-return.

label: AI Model
description: Send a prompt to an LLM (OpenAI, Anthropic, or OpenRouter) and return the generated text. Supports system prompts, temperature control, structured JSON output via an output schema, and async batch processing. The prompt field supports {{ expression }} templates to inject upstream data.

paramFields.name: model
paramFields.label: Model
paramFields.description: Models are loaded automatically based on the credential's provider

paramFields.name: prompt
paramFields.label: Prompt
paramFields.description: The main prompt to send to the model. Supports {{ expression }} templating.
paramFields.placeholder: Enter the prompt for the model...

paramFields.name: systemPrompt
paramFields.label: System Prompt
paramFields.description: Optional system prompt to set the model's behavior and context.

paramFields.name: temperature
paramFields.label: Temperature
paramFields.description: Controls randomness (0.0 to 2.0). Lower = more deterministic.

paramFields.name: maxTokens
paramFields.label: Max Tokens
paramFields.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

paramFields.name: outputJsonSchema
paramFields.label: Output JSON Schema
paramFields.description: Optional JSON Schema that constrains the model output. The model will use tool calling / structured output to return data matching this schema.

paramFields.name: useBatchProcessing
paramFields.label: Batch Processing
paramFields.description: When enabled, the prompt is submitted via the provider's batch API for asynchronous processing. The flow will pause until the batch completes.

paramFields.name: timeoutMs
paramFields.label: Timeout (ms)
paramFields.description: Per-node timeout in milliseconds. When exceeded, the SDK call is aborted and the node fails with TIMEOUT. Leave blank to use the framework default (5 minutes).

label: Flow Output
description: Return a named output value from the flow. Use as the terminal node to define what data the flow produces for callers. The output value supports {{ expression }} template syntax to reference upstream node data.

paramFields.name: outputValue
paramFields.label: Output Value
paramFields.description: The value to output from the flow. Use {{ expression }} templates to reference upstream node data.

paramFields.name: outputName
paramFields.label: Output Name
paramFields.description: Name for the output variable (default: result)

label: Switch
description: Multi-way conditional branching. Evaluates JS expressions top-to-bottom. In "first match" mode, routes to the first match or default. In "all matches" mode, activates every matching branch.

paramFields.name: matchMode
paramFields.label: Match Mode
paramFields.description: How to handle multiple matching cases. "First match" stops at the first truthy case. "All matches" activates every truthy branch.

options.label: First match
options.description: Stops at the first truthy case and routes execution to that branch only.

options.label: All matches
options.description: Activates every branch whose expression evaluates to true.

paramFields.name: cases
paramFields.label: Cases
paramFields.description: Each case has a label and a JS expression. Upstream data available as local variables by reference ID.

label: Template String
description: Replace variables in a text template using {{ expression }} syntax. Use to compose strings from upstream node outputs, flow inputs, and global config. Full JavaScript expressions are supported inside {{ }}.

paramFields.name: template
paramFields.label: Template
paramFields.description: Text with template syntax. Access incoming node data via {{ nodeId.data.variables.outputName.value }}

label: Create Folder
description:
Create a new folder in Dropbox (POST /2/files/create_folder_v2). Use when you need to organize files by creating a new directory in the user's Dropbox. Call with `path` (full path for the new folder); optional `autorename` avoids conflicts.

Example response:
```json
{"metadata": {"name": "New Folder", "path_display": "/Documents/New Folder", "id": "id:a4ayc_80_OEA..."}}
```

paramFields.name: path
paramFields.label: Path
paramFields.description: Full path for the new folder

paramFields.name: autorename
paramFields.label: Auto-rename
paramFields.description: If true, Dropbox will rename the folder if a conflict exists

label: Delete
description:
Delete a file or folder in Dropbox (POST /2/files/delete_v2). Use when you need to permanently remove a file or folder from the user's Dropbox. Call with `path` (full Dropbox path to the file or folder to delete).

Example response:
```json
{"metadata": {".tag": "file", "name": "old-file.pdf", "path_display": "/Documents/old-file.pdf", "id": "id:a4ayc_80_OEA..."}}
```

paramFields.name: path
paramFields.label: Path
paramFields.description: Full path to the file or folder to delete

label: Get Metadata
description:
Get metadata for a file or folder in Dropbox (POST /2/files/get_metadata). Use when you need file size, modification dates, or to check if a path exists. Call with `path` (full Dropbox path or file ID).

Example response:
```json
{".tag": "file", "name": "report.pdf", "path_display": "/Documents/report.pdf", "id": "id:a4ayc...", "size": 7212, "server_modified": "2025-05-12T15:50:38Z"}
```

paramFields.name: path
paramFields.label: Path
paramFields.description: Full path to the file or folder

label: List Folder
description:
List files and folders in a Dropbox directory (POST /2/files/list_folder). Use when you need to browse or enumerate the contents of a folder. Call with `path` (empty string for root), optional `limit` (1–2000, default 100), and optional `recursive` flag.

Example response:
```json
{"entries": [{".tag": "file", "name": "report.pdf", "path_display": "/Documents/report.pdf", "id": "id:a4ayc...", "size": 7212}], "has_more": false}
```

paramFields.name: path
paramFields.label: Path
paramFields.description: Folder path to list. Empty string ("") means root.

paramFields.name: limit
paramFields.label: Limit
paramFields.description: Maximum number of entries to return (1–2000)

paramFields.name: recursive
paramFields.label: Recursive
paramFields.description: If true, list contents of all sub-folders recursively

label: Search
description:
Search for files and folders in Dropbox by name or content (POST /2/files/search_v2). Use when you need to find files matching a keyword across the entire Dropbox account. Call with `query` (search string) and optional `maxResults` (1–1000, default 25).

Example response:
```json
{"matches": [{"metadata": {"metadata": {".tag": "file", "name": "report.pdf", "path_display": "/Documents/report.pdf", "id": "id:a4ayc..."}}}], "has_more": false}
```

paramFields.name: query
paramFields.label: Search Query
paramFields.description: The search query string

paramFields.name: maxResults
paramFields.label: Max Results
paramFields.description: Maximum number of results to return (1–1000)

label: Create Ticket
description:
Create a new support ticket in Freshdesk (POST /api/v2/tickets). Use when the user wants to open a ticket. Required: `subject`, `description` (HTML or plain text), and `email` (requester). Optional: `priority` (1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Urgent), `status` (2=Open, 3=Pending, 4=Resolved, 5=Closed), `type`, `tags`, `cc_emails`, `group_id`, `responder_id`. If the email doesn't exist in Freshdesk, a new contact is created.

Example response:
```json
{"id": 1, "subject": "Issue with billing", "status": 2, "priority": 1, "requester_id": 123, "group_id": null, "type": null, "tags": [], "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z"}
```

label: Get Ticket
description:
Get a Freshdesk ticket by ID (GET /api/v2/tickets/{id}). Use when the user wants to retrieve full details of a specific support ticket. Pass `include` to embed extra data: "conversations" (up to 10, costs 2 credits), "requester" (email, name, phone), "company" (id, name), "stats" (closed_at, resolved_at, first_responded_at). label: List Contacts

List contacts from a Freshdesk helpdesk (GET /api/v2/contacts). Use when the user wants to retrieve customer contact records. label: List Tickets
description:
List tickets from a Freshdesk helpdesk (GET /api/v2/tickets). Use when the user wants to retrieve a paginated list of support tickets. Supports filters: `filter` (new_and_my_open, watching, spam, deleted), `email`, `requester_id`, `updated_since` (ISO 8601). Sort with `order_by` (created_at, due_by, updated_at, status) and `order_type` (asc, desc). Default sort is `created_at desc`. label: HTTP Request
description:

label: Create Deal

label: Get Contact
description:
Get a single contact by ID from HubSpot CRM (GET /crm/v3/objects/contacts/{contactId}). Use when the user wants to look up a specific contact by their HubSpot record ID. Pass the `contactId` and optionally a comma-separated `properties` list to select which fields are returned. To look up a contact by email instead, use `hubspot.search_objects` with objectType=contacts.

Example response:
```json
{"id": "501", "properties": {"email": "jane@example.com", "firstname": "Jane", "lastname": "Doe"}, "createdAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z", "updatedAt": "2024-06-01T00:00:00.000Z", "archived": false}
```

label: List Contacts
description:
List contacts from HubSpot CRM (GET /crm/v3/objects/contacts). Use when the user wants to retrieve a paginated list of contacts. Pass `limit` (1–100) and optionally `properties` as a comma-separated list of fields to include. To paginate, use the `paging.next.after` value from the previous response as a query param in subsequent calls.

Example response:
```json
{"results": [{"id": "501", "properties": {"email": "jane@example.com", "firstname": "Jane", "lastname": "Doe"}, "createdAt": "2022-06-01T14:31:48.469Z", "updatedAt": "2025-07-07T20:27:17.947Z", "archived": false}], "paging": {"next": {"after": "502"}}}
```

paramFields.name: limit
paramFields.label: Limit
paramFields.description: Maximum number of contacts to return (1–100)

paramFields.name: properties
paramFields.label: Properties
paramFields.description: Comma-separated list of contact properties to include

label: List Deals

List deals from HubSpot CRM (GET /crm/v3/objects/deals). Use when the user wants to retrieve a paginated list of deals. Pass `limit` (1–100) and optionally `properties` as a comma-separated list of fields to include. To paginate, use the `paging.next.after` value from the previous response as a query param in subsequent calls.

```json
{"results": [{"id": "101", "properties": {"dealname": "Big deal", "amount": "50000", "dealstage": "contractsent", "closedate": "2024-12-07"}, "createdAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z", "updatedAt": "2024-06-01T00:00:00.000Z", "archived": false}], "paging": {"next": {"after": "102"}}}
```

label: Search Objects
description:
Search HubSpot CRM objects by text query (POST /crm/v3/objects/{objectType}/search). Use when the user wants to find contacts, companies, deals, or tickets matching a keyword. Pass `objectType` and a `query` string; the API searches default text properties for that object type. Results are paginated — use the `paging.next.after` value from the response to fetch subsequent pages.

Example response:
```json
{"total": 2, "results": [{"id": "501", "properties": {"email": "jane@example.com", "firstname": "Jane"}, "createdAt": "2024-01-17T19:55:04.281Z", "updatedAt": "2024-09-11T13:27:39.356Z", "archived": false}], "paging": {"next": {"after": "10"}}}
```

label: Create Page
description:
Create a new page in Notion (POST /v1/pages). Use when the user wants to add a new page under an existing page or database. Call with `parentType` ("page_id" or "database_id"), `parentId` (UUID), and `title`. Optionally pass `content` as plain text (added as a single paragraph block), `properties` as JSON for database-parent pages, and an `icon` emoji.

Example response:
```json
{"id": "new-page-uuid", "object": "page", "url": "https://notion.so/...", "created_time": "2025-04-07T10:00:00Z", "parent": {"page_id": "parent-uuid"}}
```

paramFields.name: parentType
paramFields.label: Parent Type
paramFields.description: Whether the parent is a page or a database.

paramFields.name: parentId
paramFields.label: Parent ID
paramFields.description: UUID of the parent page or database.

paramFields.name: title
paramFields.label: Title
paramFields.description: Title for the new page.

paramFields.name: content
paramFields.label: Content (Markdown)
paramFields.description: Optional plain-text content for the page body. Added as a single paragraph block. For richer content (headings, lists, etc.), use notion.append_block_children after creation.

paramFields.name: properties
paramFields.label: Properties (JSON)
paramFields.description: Additional database properties as JSON. Only used when parent is a database. The title property is set automatically.

paramFields.name: icon
paramFields.label: Icon Emoji
paramFields.description: Optional emoji icon for the page.

label: Get Page
description:
Retrieve a Notion page by ID (GET /v1/pages/{page_id}). Use when the user wants to inspect a specific page's properties, parent, or metadata. Call with `pageId` (UUID, dashes optional). Returns properties, icon, cover, parent, and timestamps — but not page content blocks (use notion.get_block_children for that).

Example response:
```json
{"id": "page-uuid", "object": "page", "url": "https://notion.so/...", "properties": {"Name": {"title": [{"plain_text": "My Page"}]}}, "parent": {"type": "workspace", "workspace": true}, "created_time": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z"}
```

paramFields.name: pageId
paramFields.label: Page ID
paramFields.description: The UUID of the Notion page to retrieve. Dashes are optional.

label: Query Database
description:
Query a Notion database with optional filters and sorts (POST /v1/databases/{database_id}/query). Use when the user wants to list, filter, or search entries in a Notion database. Call with `databaseId` (UUID). Optionally pass `filter` as a JSON object (e.g., {"property": "Status", "select": {"equals": "Done"}}) and `sorts` as a JSON array. Supports compound "and"/"or" filters. Paginate with `startCursor` and `pageSize` (1–100).

Example response:
```json
{"results": [{"id": "page-uuid", "properties": {"Name": {"title": [{"plain_text": "Task 1"}]}, "Status": {"select": {"name": "Done"}}}}], "has_more": false, "next_cursor": null}
```

paramFields.name: databaseId
paramFields.label: Database ID
paramFields.description: UUID of the Notion database to query.

paramFields.name: filter
paramFields.label: Filter (JSON)
paramFields.description: Notion filter object as JSON. Supports compound "and"/"or" filters. See Notion docs for filter syntax.

paramFields.name: sorts
paramFields.label: Sorts (JSON)
paramFields.description: Array of sort objects. Each has "property" + "direction" ("ascending"/"descending"), or "timestamp" + "direction".

paramFields.name: pageSize
paramFields.label: Page Size
paramFields.description: Number of results to return (1–100).

paramFields.name: startCursor
paramFields.label: Start Cursor
paramFields.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

label: Search
description:
Search pages and databases in Notion by title (POST /v1/search). Use when the user wants to find a specific page or database in their workspace. Call with an optional `query` string to match titles; use `filterType` to restrict to "page" or "database"; results are sorted by `last_edited_time` (descending by default). Supports pagination via `startCursor` and `pageSize` (1–100).

Example response:
```json
{"results": [{"id": "page-uuid", "object": "page", "url": "https://notion.so/...", "created_time": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z", "last_edited_time": "2025-04-01T12:00:00Z"}], "has_more": false, "next_cursor": null}
```

paramFields.name: sortDirection
paramFields.label: Sort Direction
paramFields.description: Sort by last edited time.

paramFields.name: pageSize
paramFields.label: Page Size
paramFields.description: Number of results to return (1–100).

paramFields.name: startCursor
paramFields.label: Start Cursor
paramFields.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

label: Update Page

Update a Notion page's properties or metadata (PATCH /v1/pages/{page_id}). Use when the user wants to rename a page, change its properties, icon, or archive/un-archive it. label: Group User
description:
Associate a user with a group via Segment (POST /v1/group). Use when the user wants to link a user to a company, team, or account and set group-level traits. Call with `userId`, `groupId`, and an optional `traits` JSON object (e.g. `{"name": "Acme Corp", "plan": "enterprise"}`). Note: only `userId` is supported; `anonymousId`-only calls are not yet available.

Example response:
```json
{"acknowledged": true, "userId": "user-123", "groupId": "group-456"}
```

label: Identify User
description:
Identify a user and set traits via Segment (POST /v1/identify). Use when the user wants to associate profile attributes like email, name, or plan with a user ID. Call with `userId` and an optional `traits` JSON object (e.g. `{"email": "...", "plan": "pro"}`). An optional ISO 8601 `timestamp` overrides the server default. Note: only `userId` is supported; `anonymousId`-only calls are not yet available.

Example response:
```json
{"acknowledged": true, "userId": "user-123"}
```

label: Page View
description:
Record a page view for a user via Segment (POST /v1/page). Use when the user wants to track which pages a user visits along with page metadata. Call with `userId`, `name` (page name, e.g. "Home"), and an optional `properties` JSON object (e.g. `{"url": "...", "referrer": "..."}`). Note: only `userId` is supported; `anonymousId`-only calls are not yet available.

```json
{"acknowledged": true, "userId": "user-123", "name": "Home"}
```

label: Track Event
description:
Track an analytics event for a user via Segment (POST /v1/track). Use when the user wants to record a business event like a purchase, signup, or feature usage. Call with `userId`, `event` (e.g. "Order Completed"), and an optional `properties` JSON object with event-specific data. An optional ISO 8601 `timestamp` overrides the server default. Note: only `userId` is supported; `anonymousId`-only calls are not yet available.


label: Get Email Activity
description:
Query the SendGrid Email Activity Feed (GET /v3/messages). Call with an optional `query` filter string (e.g. `to_email="user@example.com"`, `status="delivered"`, `subject="Hello"`) and `limit` (1–1000, default 10). Use when the user wants to track delivery status of specific emails — delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, deferred, etc. Requires an API key with Email Activity Feed access enabled.

Example response:

```json
{"messages": [{"msg_id": "abc123", "from_email": "sender@example.com", "to_email": "user@example.com", "subject": "Hello", "status": "delivered", "opens_count": 2, "clicks_count": 1, "last_event_time": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"}]}
```

label: Get Email Stats
description:
Retrieve global email sending statistics (GET /v3/stats). Call with `startDate` (YYYY-MM-DD, required), optional `endDate`, and `aggregatedBy` (day/week/month). Use when the user wants to analyze email performance metrics over a date range — requests, deliveries, opens, clicks, bounces, spam reports, and unique counts.

Example response:
```json
[{"date": "2024-01-01", "stats": [{"metrics": {"requests": 100, "delivered": 98, "opens": 45, "unique_opens": 30, "clicks": 12, "bounces": 2, "spam_reports": 0}}]}]
```

label: Send Email
description:
Send a transactional email via SendGrid (POST /v3/mail/send). Call with `to`, `subject`, and either `html`/`text` body or a `templateId` with `dynamicTemplateData`. Supports `cc`, `bcc`, `replyTo`, and scheduled sending via `sendAt` (Unix timestamp, up to 72h ahead). The `from` address must be a verified sender or domain.

Returns HTTP 202 Accepted with no JSON body. The `x-message-id` header contains the tracking ID.

Example output:
```json
{"message": "Email sent successfully to user@example.com", "messageId": "abc123def456", "statusCode": 202}
```

label: Create Card
description:
Create a new card in a Trello list (POST /1/cards). Use when the user wants to add a task or item to a Trello board.

Example response:
```json
{"id": "card123", "name": "New feature", "desc": "", "due": null, "url": "https://trello.com/c/H0TZyzbK", "idList": "list123"}
```

label: List Boards

List all Trello boards accessible by the authenticated user (GET /1/members/me/boards). Use when the user needs to find board IDs before listing lists or cards.

```json
[{"id": "board123", "name": "Project Alpha", "desc": "Main board", "url": "https://trello.com/b/abc123", "closed": false}]
```

label: Cron Trigger
description:
Start this flow on a recurring schedule using a cron expression. This is a flow entry-point node — AI agents should not invoke it directly. Configure the cron expression (5-field standard, e.g. "0 9 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 9 AM) and an IANA timezone in the flow editor. Optionally set static inputs to include with every trigger.

Output shape:
```json
{"scheduledTime": "2025-01-15T09:00:00.000Z", "expression": "0 9 * * 1-5", "timezone": "America/New_York", "staticInputs": {}}
```

paramFields.name: expression
paramFields.label: Cron Expression
paramFields.description: Cron schedule (e.g. "0 * * * *" for every hour, "0 9 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 9am)

paramFields.name: timezone
paramFields.label: Timezone
paramFields.description: IANA timezone (e.g. "America/New_York", "Europe/London"). Defaults to UTC.

paramFields.name: staticInputs
paramFields.label: Static Inputs
paramFields.description: Optional JSON object of static values to include with every cron trigger

label: Manual Trigger
description:
Start this flow manually from the UI, REST API, or programmatically via startFlowRun(). Optionally set Default Inputs — a JSON object of key/value pairs used when the flow is triggered manually. When triggered from code, the caller's values are used instead (merged on top of defaults). This is a flow entry-point node — AI agents should not invoke it directly.

Example default inputs:
```json
{ "topic": "hello world", "severity": "medium" }
```

paramFields.name: defaultInputs
paramFields.label: Default Inputs
paramFields.description: Default input values used when running the flow manually. A plain JSON object — e.g. { "topic": "hello world" }. When triggered from code, the caller's values take precedence.

label: Create Tweet
description:
Post a new tweet to the authenticated user's timeline (POST /2/tweets). Use when the user wants to publish a tweet or status update on X/Twitter. Text is limited to 280 characters.

Example response:
```json
{"data": {"id": "1346889436626259968", "text": "Hello world!"}}
```

paramFields.name: text
paramFields.label: Tweet Text
paramFields.description: The text content of the tweet (max 280 characters)

label: Get Me
description:
Get the authenticated Twitter/X user's profile (GET /2/users/me). Use when the user wants to check their own account details, bio, or follower counts.

Example response:
```json
{"data": {"id": "2244994945", "name": "X Dev", "username": "xdev", "public_metrics": {"followers_count": 5000, "tweet_count": 1200}}}
```

label: Get User
description:
Look up a Twitter/X user profile by username (GET /2/users/by/username/:username). Use when the user wants to find information about a specific Twitter account.

Example response:
```json
{"data": {"id": "2244994945", "name": "X Developers", "username": "XDevelopers", "description": "The voice of the X developer community", "public_metrics": {"followers_count": 583423}}}
```

paramFields.name: username
paramFields.label: Username
paramFields.description: Twitter username (without the @ symbol)

label: Get User Tweets
description:
Get recent tweets posted by a Twitter/X user (GET /2/users/:id/tweets). Use when the user wants to see what a specific account has been posting.

Example response:
```json
{"data": [{"id": "123", "text": "Hello", "created_at": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", "public_metrics": {"like_count": 10, "retweet_count": 2}}], "meta": {"result_count": 5}}
```

paramFields.name: userId
paramFields.label: User ID
paramFields.description: The numeric Twitter user ID. Use Get User to find a user ID from a username.

paramFields.name: maxResults
paramFields.label: Max Results
paramFields.description: Maximum number of tweets to return (1–100)

label: Search Tweets
description:
Search recent tweets matching a query (GET /2/tweets/search/recent). Use when the user wants to find tweets about a topic, hashtag, or from a specific account.

Example response:
```json
{"data": [{"id": "123", "text": "Hello", "author_id": "456", "public_metrics": {"like_count": 5}}], "meta": {"result_count": 10}}
```

paramFields.name: query
paramFields.label: Search Query
paramFields.description: Twitter search query. Supports operators like from:, to:, #hashtag, "exact phrase", etc.

paramFields.name: maxResults
paramFields.label: Max Results
paramFields.description: Maximum number of tweets to return (10–100)

label: Create Product
description:
Create a new product in a WooCommerce store (POST /wp-json/wc/v3/products). Use when you need to add a new product to the catalog. Call with `name` and `regularPrice` (required); optional `type`, `status`, `sku`, `salePrice`, `stockQuantity`, `description`, and `shortDescription`.

Example response:
```json
{"id": 794, "name": "Premium Quality T-Shirt", "type": "simple", "status": "publish", "sku": "WOO-TSHIRT-001", "regular_price": "21.99", "permalink": "https://example.com/product/premium-quality-t-shirt"}
```

paramFields.name: name
paramFields.label: Product Name
paramFields.description: The product name

paramFields.name: regularPrice
paramFields.label: Regular Price
paramFields.description: Product regular price as a string (e.g. "29.99")

paramFields.name: type
paramFields.label: Product Type
paramFields.description: The type of product

paramFields.name: description
paramFields.label: Description
paramFields.description: Full product description (HTML supported)

paramFields.name: shortDescription
paramFields.label: Short Description
paramFields.description: Brief product summary (HTML supported)

paramFields.name: status
paramFields.label: Status
paramFields.description: Product publication status

paramFields.name: sku
paramFields.label: SKU
paramFields.description: Stock keeping unit (unique product identifier)

paramFields.name: salePrice
paramFields.label: Sale Price
paramFields.description: Product sale price as a string (e.g. "19.99")

paramFields.name: stockQuantity
paramFields.label: Stock Quantity
paramFields.description: Number of items in stock (enables stock management)

label: Get Product
description:
Get a single product by ID from a WooCommerce store (GET /wp-json/wc/v3/products/{id}). Use when you need full details for a specific product including pricing, stock, categories, and attributes. Call with `productId` (numeric product ID).

Example response:
```json
{"id": 794, "name": "Premium Quality T-Shirt", "type": "simple", "status": "publish", "sku": "WOO-TSHIRT-001", "regular_price": "21.99", "sale_price": "", "stock_quantity": 15, "stock_status": "instock", "categories": [{"id": 9, "name": "Clothing"}]}
```

paramFields.name: productId
paramFields.label: Product ID
paramFields.description: The numeric ID of the product to retrieve

label: List Customers
description:
List customers from a WooCommerce store (GET /wp-json/wc/v3/customers). Use when you need to look up customer accounts, contact details, or billing/shipping info. Call with optional `search` (keyword matching name/username), `email` (exact email filter), and `perPage` (1–100).

Example response:
```json
[{"id": 25, "email": "john@example.com", "first_name": "John", "last_name": "Doe", "billing": {"city": "San Francisco", "state": "CA"}, "orders_count": 3, "total_spent": "89.97"}]
```

paramFields.description: Maximum number of customers to return (1–100)

paramFields.label: Search
paramFields.description: Keyword to search customers by name or username

paramFields.name: email
paramFields.label: Email
paramFields.description: Filter customers by exact email address

label: List Orders
description:
List orders from a WooCommerce store (GET /wp-json/wc/v3/orders). Use when you need to review recent orders, check payment or fulfillment status, or search for specific orders. Call with optional `status` (pending, processing, on-hold, completed, cancelled, refunded, failed), `search` (keyword), and `perPage` (1–100).

Example response:
```json
[{"id": 727, "status": "processing", "total": "29.99", "currency": "USD", "date_created": "2024-01-15T10:30:00", "billing": {"first_name": "John", "email": "john@example.com"}, "line_items": [{"name": "T-Shirt", "quantity": 1}]}]
```

label: List Products

label: Create Ticket
description:
Create a new support ticket in Zendesk (POST /api/v2/tickets). Use when the user wants to open a support ticket. Call with `subject` and `description` (ticket body text); optionally set `priority` (urgent/high/normal/low), `type` (problem/incident/question/task), and `requesterEmail`.

Example response:
```json
{"id": 35436, "subject": "Help!", "status": "new", "priority": "normal", "requester_id": 123, "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"}
```

paramFields.name: subdomain
paramFields.label: Subdomain
paramFields.description: Your Zendesk subdomain (the part before .zendesk.com)

paramFields.name: subject
paramFields.label: Subject
paramFields.description: The subject line of the ticket
paramFields.placeholder: e.g. Cannot log in to my account

paramFields.name: description
paramFields.label: Description
paramFields.description: The full description of the ticket. Supports {{ expression }} templating.

paramFields.name: priority
paramFields.label: Priority
paramFields.description: Ticket priority level.

paramFields.name: type
paramFields.label: Type
paramFields.description: Ticket type.

paramFields.name: requesterEmail
paramFields.label: Requester Email
paramFields.description: Email of the ticket requester. Leave empty to use the authenticated user.

label: Get Ticket
description:
Get a single Zendesk ticket by ID (GET /api/v2/tickets/{ticketId}). Use when the user wants to retrieve full details of a specific support ticket including its status, priority, requester, assignee, tags, and description. Call with `ticketId`.

Example response:
```json
{"id": 35436, "subject": "Help!", "status": "open", "priority": "normal", "requester_id": 123, "assignee_id": 235323, "tags": ["billing"], "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"}
```

paramFields.name: subdomain
paramFields.label: Subdomain
paramFields.description: Your Zendesk subdomain (the part before .zendesk.com)

paramFields.name: ticketId
paramFields.label: Ticket ID
paramFields.description: The numeric ID of the ticket to retrieve

label: List Tickets
description:
List tickets from a Zendesk instance (GET /api/v2/tickets). Use when the user wants to retrieve a paginated list of support tickets sorted by newest first. Call with optional `perPage` (1–100, default 25). Note: does not support query filtering — use `zendesk.search` instead.

Example response:
```json
{"tickets": [{"id": 35436, "subject": "Help!", "status": "open", "priority": "normal", "requester_id": 123}], "count": 1, "meta": {"has_more": true}}
```

paramFields.name: subdomain
paramFields.label: Subdomain
paramFields.description: Your Zendesk subdomain (the part before .zendesk.com)

paramFields.name: perPage
paramFields.label: Results Per Page
paramFields.description: Number of tickets to return (1–100).

label: Search
description:
Search Zendesk tickets, users, or organizations (GET /api/v2/search.json). Use when the user wants to find resources by criteria. Call with `query` using Zendesk search syntax — e.g. `type:ticket status:open`, `type:ticket priority:urgent assignee:me`, `type:user email:foo@bar.com`. Optional `perPage` (1–100).

Example response:
```json
{"results": [{"id": 35436, "result_type": "ticket", "subject": "Help!", "status": "open", "priority": "high"}], "count": 5, "nextPage": "https://..."  }
```

paramFields.name: subdomain
paramFields.label: Subdomain
paramFields.description: Your Zendesk subdomain (the part before .zendesk.com)

paramFields.name: query
paramFields.label: Search Query
paramFields.description: Zendesk search query. Supports syntax like type:ticket, status:open, assignee:me, priority:urgent, created>2024-01-01, tags:billing.

paramFields.name: perPage
paramFields.label: Results Per Page
paramFields.description: Number of results to return (1–100).

label: Update Ticket
description:
Update a Zendesk ticket (PUT /api/v2/tickets/{ticketId}). Use when the user wants to change a ticket’s status, priority, subject, or add a comment. Call with `ticketId` and at least one of: `status` (new/open/pending/hold/solved/closed), `priority`, `subject`, or `comment` (with optional `commentPublic` flag for internal notes).

Example response:
```json
{"id": 35436, "subject": "Help!", "status": "solved", "priority": "normal", "updated_at": "2024-01-16T14:30:00Z"}
```
